definition: Dedollarization is real at the margin in reserve allocation (declining 0.5-0.8pp/year) but tactically irrelevant against $7.5T daily FX turnover. The critical analytical distinction is between four dimensions: reserve allocation (declining), trade invoicing (stable), financial intermediation (stable-to-growing), and settlement infrastructure (challenged). USD remains involved in 88% of FX turnover, unchanged over 20 years.
evidence: CNY reserve share plateaued at 2.3-2.7% since 2022
CIPS growth ($6T→$14T) likely inflated by domestic volumes
Gold purchases (1,000+ tonnes/year) better explained by portfolio rebalancing and sanctions-proofing
Sterling-to-dollar transition took 30+ years (1914-1944) requiring conditions China does not yet meet
pair_3 called the four-dimensional decomposition Agent B's strongest contribution
open_questions: At what pace could CNY reserve share accelerate if China opens capital account?
Does settlement infrastructure challenge (CIPS) create meaningful friction for dollar alternatives?
What threshold of reserve share decline becomes tactically relevant?
